Job Title: Senior Executive, General Medicine (Clinical Epidemiology Unit)
Job Description: ***This is a full-time position offered on a 2-year employment contract, with the possibility of renewal/extension based on performance and organisational needs.The Senior Executive will be responsible to monitor clinical surveillance data and prepare reports to track the occurrence of infectious diseases in the institution. Contact trace individuals who have been possibly exposed to infectious diseases within the institution.Summarise relevant clinical information for reporting, evaluation and mitigation of infectious diseases exposure risks within institution. MAJOR DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES (A)    SPECIFIC1. Maintain accurate symptom and disease surveillance data record in a timely manner. (E.g. COVID-19 surveillance, Severe Influenza surveillance, Group A Streptococcus surveillance, TB surveillance, MOH Severe Illness and Deaths from Possible Infectious Causes (SIDPIC))2. Conduct basic epidemiological or operational data analysis for surveillance reporting of infectious diseases to relevant parties with good accuracy and timeliness.3. Gather and summarise relevant clinical information of patient index for reporting, evaluation, and mitigation of infectious diseases exposure within the institution.4. Coordinate contact tracing efforts with the relevant parties and complete the case investigation in a timely manner. Ensure timely and accurate recording of information of contacts and actions based on operational guidelines and procedures.5. Conduct investigations and interviews in a professional and empathetic manner to trace those in close contact with the diagnosed case and to determine the exposure.6. Collaborate with internal departments and regulatory bodies in supporting the screening and/or isolation of exposed contacts.7. Provide information on testing and or isolation/quarantine procedures based on protocols and procedures. Monitor and keep record of the symptoms and/or test results of contacts identified, as required.(B)    GENERAL1. Assist with unit’s administrative and operation duties.2. Participation in department’s education program / journal clubs.3. Embrace continuous improvement through refinement of work performance.4. Any other projects, tasks and duties as assigned by Head of Clinical Epidemiology Unit or the Chairman Medical Board (CMB).JOB REQUIREMENTSEDUCATION, TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE1. Recognised Degree in any discipline.2. Proficient in Microsoft Office (E.g. Excel, Word, PowerPoint)3. Experience with data analysis using statistical software (E.g. STATA, R, Python, SAS, SPSS, Epi Info) will be advantageous.4. Good written and verbal communication skills.5. Ability to handle confidential information with discretion and professionalism.

Job Title: Executive/ Senior Executive , Ops Resilience & Knowledge Management Cell
Job Description: Job Role: Executive / Senior Executive, Ops Resilience and Knowledge Management (ORKM), Integrated Operations Hub (IOH)Job PurposeSupport the Integrated Ops division to develop and upkeep the operational readiness of IOps, to ensure that IOps can continue to deliver business as usual / critical operations amid disruptions, whether from internal failures, cyber-attacks, natural disasters or any unexpected events.As part of Integrated Ops Hub, ORKM provides the risk assessment for upcoming major works /events and potential threats to the island and formulates the necessary risk management plan.Ensure that knowledge and learnings from incidents / exercises / events are properly documented, stored, maintained and reviewed as required.Duties & Responsibilities – Ops ResiliencePart of the custodian team for the Sentosa Resilience Framework, and uphold the responsibilities for risk identification, risk mitigation, incident response and incident recovery. To also assist to review the framework where required.Part of the Sentosa Crisis Operations Group (SCOG), to assume the role of Crisis Secretariat to facilitate backend support work such as coordination, push and pull of information to/from various SCOG Cells and Incident Management teams (IMT). The role also includes preparation of situational reports to Senior Management, and hosting of crisis meetings, as directed by Head SCOG.Environmental scanning to identify potential operational risks and threats in the context of Sentosa; collaboration with various stakeholders to ensure relevant incident response / drawer plans are developed to address the risks. Review and draw lessons from external incidences which are applicable to Sentosa and ensure that risks identifications are aligned with Sentosa’s overall Enterprise Risk Matrix.Assist the ORKM team to have an overview of crisis management exercises being conducted on the island and plan / conduct / coordinate and collaborate with stakeholders where appropriate, including the summary exercise for Sentosa. After the exercises have concluded, to assess potential gaps in the operational processes and to trigger the relevant mitigation required to address the gaps.Strategic Ops Planning / review of operational processes based on data driven decisions and gap analysis.Networking with internal and external stakeholders / agencies to build relationships for collaborations, information / knowledge sharing and to level up Sentosa situational awareness both internally and externally. Liaison between SDC and all government agencies to facilitate official inspection, investigations and enforcement matters.To develop Community Policing/engagement program, ensure stakeholders are aligned and updated on the Emergency Preparedness SOPs.Secretariat role for key operational meeting platforms such as, but not limited to:                                i.            Sentosa Island Working Group (SIWG)                                ii.            Safety and Security Review Committee (SSRC)                             iii.            Integrated Operations Coordination Meeting (IOCM)                              iv.            Management Cluster Walk                               v.            Operations Readiness Plan (ORP)                             vi.            After Action ReviewsResponsible for building up and maintaining the central depository and ensuring documentation is done for learnings from AARs, SOPs, manuals etc, either by IOH or the respective subject matter experts.Custodian of SOPs / Manuals etc, to ensure that content / knowledge that is documented remains relevant to the current context. Collaboration with the relevant stakeholders to update and review the documents as required.Attend community functions as required.Preparation of management reports for Board, DM, and department updates.Any other tasks as assigned by Reporting Officer.RequirementsDegree in any discipline.Have working experience at business continuity management and/or crisis management.Comfortable with working / networking with multiple stakeholders and managing multiple streams of work / project at once.Able to adapt well to changing environments and deliver under high stress situations.Good time management and organization skills is a must.Able to manage and protect highly confidential and proprietary information.Able to be deployed for events/duty on weekends/PH, and to be support crisis operations when required.

COMMUNITY CARE ASSOCIATEWe are seeking a passionate and resourceful Community Care Associate to join our Centre-Based Services team. In this role, you will play a vital part in enhancing the quality of life for our elders by monitoring their health status and providing direct care, as well as planning and conducting meaningful activities based on their preferences and interests. You will take ownership of supporting individual clients well-being and contributing to the overall engagement experience within the Centre.ResponsibilitiesSupport seniors with basic activities of daily living (BADL), including personal hygiene and mobility assistance.Identify and respond to seniors care needs, including dietary and overall well-being.Monitor health indicators such as vital signs, glucose levels, and oxygen saturation, and escalate concerns appropriately.Assist with serving and administering pre-packed and topical medication as directed.Plan and conduct engaging activities tailored to seniors preferences and interests.Observe and document seniors participation and responses during activities.Maintain a clean, safe, and hygienic environment within the centre, including regular cleaning and hazard checks.Support other assigned projects and tasks as delegated by the manager.RequirementsMinimum Higher Nitec qualifications.Genuine interest and patience in serving elderly clients.Prior caregiving experience in hospitals, nursing homes, or home settings is an advantage.Open to candidates exploring a career switch into eldercare.Strong team player with good communication and organisational skills; able to work independently under time constraints.Proficient in Microsoft Office and ExcelAbout St Lukes EldercareSt Lukes ElderCare (SLEC) is a Christian healthcare provider dedicated to enriching the lives of seniors in Singapore, regardless of race, language and religion.Guided by our GRACE philosophy of care, we are committed to providing compassionate and holistic care that fosters autonomy and choice. To empower elders of varying needs, from the fit to the frail, we offer a comprehensive suite of services islandwide. These include community-based programmes that promote active ageing; centre-based offerings such as day care, rehabilitation and nursing; residential (nursing home) services for long-term care; and home-based services covering medical, nursing and therapy needs.Leveraging our legacy of over 25 years, we are on an unstoppable mission to transform care challenges of Singapores ageing population. Through innovation, collaboration and education, we seek to elevate the community care sector, where elders thrive in their golden years and age with dignity, independence and joy.Incorporated in 1999, SLEC is a registered charity and an Institution of a Public Character.

Job Title: (Senior) Executive / (Senior) Assistant Manager, Diagnostic Imaging IT
Job Description: Job descriptionCareer in the HospitalJoin our incredible team at the National University Hospital (NUH), where we believe that a hospital is more than just a workplace. Every day, we witness the remarkable impact that our healthcare professionals can have on individuals, families, and entire communities.At NUH, we are driven by a shared purpose – to lend a helping hand to those in need, to be a source of support and healing, and to empower patients to live their best lives. We are looking for passionate and committed individuals to join us as we transform lives and create a lasting impact in the lives of our patients. The impact you make at NUH will be profound, regardless of the role you take up in the hospital.About NUHThe National University Hospital (NUH) is Singapore’s leading university hospital, with a proud heritage dating back to 1905 with the establishment of what’s now the NUS Yong Loo Lin School of Medicine. Our strong ties with the medical school have shaped us into a unique institution, allowing us to seamlessly integrate clinical care with teaching and research. As a university hospital, we go beyond the realm of providing tertiary medical care. We are a hub of knowledge and innovation, attracting healthcare professionals who are passionate about pushing boundaries and making significant contributions to the field.About Department and Purpose of the PositionThis role serves as the operational bridge between Radiological clinical services and IT systems, ensuring that technology solutions are aligned with clinical workflows and service delivery. The Radiology IT Operations & Projects Administrator is responsible for managing and executing informatics initiatives, including AI platforms, imaging technologies, and digital systems. By coordinating across Radiology, Informatics, and IT teams, the position ensures secure, efficient, and scalable integration of systems that support diagnostic imaging operations. The role also oversees contract governance, system renewals, and intra-cluster service arrangements, while driving process improvements and supporting staff competency in Radiology IT workflows.Key Responsibilities1. Project Management and Operations (70% of time):•Lead operationalization and management of Radiology IT projects including AI technologies, 3D imaging, 3D printing, and other emerging platforms.•Track and manage Medical Devices and Operational Technology Security (MDOTS) compliance, IT RA Deviations, and renewals for Radiology imaging scanners and IT applications•Responsible for Contract lifecycle and management, ensuring timely renewals or terminations for all Radiology IT Systems•Assist with Process Change Request (PCR) and Operation System Request (OSR) preparation and User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for electronic medical records system (EPIC) to support Radiology IT.•Track & Coordinate project timelines and deliverables with Radiology Informatics personnel.•Organize and facilitate troubleshooting sessions with relevant IT teams.•Develop and maintain user guides, SOPs, and training materials to enhance staff competency.•To support budgeting and procurement processes for Radiology IT projects and systems.2. Intra-cluster Processes and Services (25% of time):•Manage and optimize intra-cluster referral processes•Oversee leasing arrangements for equipment and services•Coordinate outsourcing of services as needed3. Data Analysis and Process Improvement (5% of time):•Conduct data analysis to identify areas for improvement in departmental processes•Propose and implement process improvements based on data-driven insights.4. Perform any other duties assigned by supervisorKey RequirementsQualification Required and Area of Discipline:•Degree or Diploma in Business IT / Information Technology/ Diagnostic Radiography/ Health Informatics / a related fields.•This is a full-time permanent position.Required Competencies and Capabilities (Skills, Experiences, and Professional Licenses)•Preferred at least 2 years of specialized or relevant experience in contract management and vendor relations or Information Technology environment (IT professionals) or project management, preferably in a healthcare setting. However, fresh graduates are encouraged to apply too.•Basic understanding of IT and network systems.Job-related Competencies and Capabilities•Proficient with Microsoft Word, Excel, Powerpoint•Strong interpersonal, communication and written skills•Self-motivated, adaptable, and able to work independently or in teams.•Flexible hours; some duties require after-office work.

Job Title: SCDF Paramedic Specialist (Work-Study Diploma in Paramedicine)
Job Description: What the role isThe Paramedic Specialist (Work-Study Diploma in Paramedicine) programme by SCDF offers an excellent option for individuals navigating the post-ITE graduation phase, providing a blend of academic pursuit and practical work experience, thus offering the benefits of both realms. Job Title: (Senior) Assistant Manager, Medical Affairs - Clinical Governance (MACG)

The smiles of gratitude from patients and the profound sense of fulfilment that comes from helping others cannot be measured. It is a career that allows for both personal and professional growth. The challenges you encounter in this rapidly evolving industry push you to constantly expand your knowledge and skills, as well as encourage lifelong learning.Be a part of a team that gets to create a transformative healthcare experience for our patients and professionals alike.
